candidates.name: Katie George
candidates.ai_summary: Katie George is a certified Utah teacher with 14 years of lead teaching experience, specializing in early childhood and elementary education. She is available full-time or part-time within a 15-mile radius of Pleasant Grove, Utah, and employs a restorative approach to conflict resolution.

transcripts.q3: Hi, my name is Katie George. I'm a certified teacher for the state of Utah. I have a bachelor's degree in elementary education and the dual major in early childhood education. I predominantly work with children between the ages of 3, to 7 years old. And I especially enjoy working with preschool age children because it's such a critical stage of development. And it's such a pleasure to make learning fun, and meaningful for them and see student grow academically. And personally,
transcripts.q4: I live in Pleasant, Grove Utah, and I'm willing to travel about 15 miles each day.
transcripts.q5: I'm open to part-time or full-time. I'm available Monday through Friday, from about 8, a.m. to 5 p.m.
transcripts.q6: In regard to two children fighting with one another, I would immediately intervene and talk to them about why their behavior was unacceptable and the importance of kindness and respect. I would help the students self-regulate and explain that it's important to take time to calm down and come back to talk about what happened and find their own solutions. To the problem, I would be sure to acknowledge any efforts that were made and use positive reinforcement.
transcripts.q7: I have 14 years of experience working as a lead teacher at Early Learning Essentials formerly known as Head. Start, I taught kindergarten for a year and I feel comfortable and confident working with preschool age children, and Phil, I'd be a good asset to the team.
